Next, I will proceed to open the title and abstract anchor. {"action":"open","anchor_id":"DOC2_A1","expected_evidence":"title and abstract of the paper"} # InMoDeGAN: Interpretable Motion Decomposition Generative Adversarial Network for Video Generation Yaohui Wang Francois Bremond Antitza Dantcheva
Inria, Université Côte d'Azur
\{yaohui.wang, francois.bremond, antitza.dantcheva\}@inria.fr
https://wyhsirius.github.io/InMoDeGAN / (a) ![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/2024_06_04_7b79a73b3f86ab3d9176g-01.jpg?height=465&width=839&top_left_y=792&top_left_x=163) (b) ![](https://cdn.mathpix.com/cropped/2024_06_04_7b79a73b3f86ab3d9176g-01.jpg?height=452&width=830&top_left_y=796&top_left_x=1032) Figure 1: Controllable video generation. InMoDeGAN learns to decompose motion into semantic motion-components. This allows for manipulations in the latent code to invoke motion in generated videos that is human interpretable. Top (a) robot arm moves backwards, bottom (a) robot arm moves to the right. Similarly, in (b) we are animating the face to 'talk' (top) and 'move head' (bottom). #### Abstract --- Round 2 --- The previous block contained the introduction and background section of the paper.
